description: A suite of volatility measurement and price band indicators for quantifying market risk, setting dynamic stop levels, and identifying breakout conditions across financial time series

fields.name: real_prices
fields.label: Price series — typically Close (required for BBANDS, STDDEV, VAR, AVGDEV)

fields.name: high_prices
fields.label: High Prices (required for ATR, NATR, TRANGE, BETA-like range studies)

fields.name: low_prices
fields.label: Low Prices (required for ATR, NATR, TRANGE)

fields.name: close_prices
fields.label: Close Prices (required for ATR, NATR, TRANGE — previous close for True Range)

fields.name: real0
fields.label: First price series for BETA / CORREL (e.g., asset returns)

fields.name: real1
fields.label: Second price series for BETA / CORREL (e.g., benchmark returns)

fields.name: time_period
fields.label: Time Period (default by indicator: ATR=14, NATR=14, BBANDS=5, STDDEV=5, VAR=5, AVGDEV=14, BETA=5, CORREL=30)

fields.name: nb_dev_up
fields.label: BBANDS Upper Band Deviation Multiplier (default 2.0; any real number)

fields.name: nb_dev_dn
fields.label: BBANDS Lower Band Deviation Multiplier (default 2.0; any real number)

fields.name: ma_type
fields.label: BBANDS Middle Band MA Type (default SMA)

fields.name: nb_dev
fields.label: Standard Deviation / Variance multiplier for STDDEV/VAR output scaling (default 1.0)

fields.name: out_values
fields.label: Primary output array (ATR, NATR, TRANGE, STDDEV, VAR, AVGDEV, BETA, CORREL values)

fields.name: out_middle_band
fields.label: BBANDS Middle Band values (MA of price)

true_range.formula: TR = max(High - Low, abs(High - PrevClose), abs(Low - PrevClose))
true_range.edge_case: First bar has no PrevClose; TR = High - Low for bar[0]

atr.formula: ATR[0] = mean(TR[0..n-1]); ATR[i] = (ATR[i-1] * (n-1) + TR[i]) / n  (Wilder smoothing)

use_cases.position_sizing: Risk per trade = account_risk / ATR — position size adapts to volatility
use_cases.stop_loss: Stop distance = N * ATR (Chandelier Exit, Keltner Channels)
use_cases.trailing_stop: Trail by 2× or 3× ATR below highest close

atr.note: Wilder smoothing is equivalent to EMA with alpha = 1/n (slower than standard EMA alpha 2/(n+1))

natr.advantage: Cross-instrument comparable — NATR of 1.5% on a $10 stock and a $1000 stock are equivalent volatility
natr.edge_case: If Close = 0, NATR is undefined (division by zero)

bbands.band_width: bandwidth = (UpperBand - LowerBand) / MiddleBand — normalized volatility measure
bbands.percent_b: %b = (price - LowerBand) / (UpperBand - LowerBand) — price position within bands

interpretation.squeeze: Narrowing bands indicate low volatility / pre-breakout compression
interpretation.breakout: Price closing above UpperBand or below LowerBand signals expansion
interpretation.walk_the_band: Price can trend along a band; closure across does not automatically mean reversal

bbands.statistical_basis: With SMA and nbDev=2, ~95% of prices fall within bands under normal distribution

stddev.formula: STDDEV[i] = sqrt(sum((price[j] - mean(price, n))^2, j=i-n+1..i) / n) * nbDev

use_cases.volatility_filter: High StdDev = noisy market; low StdDev = consolidation
use_cases.bbands_construction: BBANDS internally calls STDDEV for band width calculation

variance.note: Variance is STDDEV squared; use when downstream statistical models need variance (e.g., portfolio optimization)

avgdev.formula: AVGDEV[i] = sum(abs(price[j] - mean(price, n)), j=i-n+1..i) / n
avgdev.note: More robust than STDDEV to outliers; used internally by CCI's denominator

beta.formula: Uses slope of linear regression of real0 vs real1 over the period
beta.output: Rolling beta coefficient (typically >1 = more volatile than benchmark)
beta.use_cases: Portfolio risk decomposition; hedge ratio calculation

correl.formula: CORREL = cov(real0, real1, n) / (stddev(real0, n) * stddev(real1, n))
correl.use_cases: Pair trading; portfolio diversification; asset correlation measurement

then.value: first input index mapped to out[0]

compute_success.result: Volatility series aligned to input via outBegIdx

bbands_computed.result: Three overlaid bands; bandwidth contraction (squeeze) precedes breakout

volatility_expansion.result: Volatility expansion signal — widen stops or reduce position size

insufficient_data.result: No valid output; buffer more bars

any: time_period < 2 and indicator_type in [BBANDS, STDDEV, VAR]
nb_dev_up == 0 and nb_dev_dn == 0 and indicator_type == BBANDS

invalid_parameters.result: Function returns error code; validate parameters before calling

natr_zero_close.result: NATR output undefined at that bar; caller must filter zero-price bars

errors.message: Not enough historical bars. ATR requires at least timePeriod bars; BBANDS requires timePeriod.
A parameter is outside its valid range (period < 1 for ATR/NATR, < 2 for BBANDS/STDDEV).
A price value is zero or non-finite, causing division by zero in NATR.

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use BBANDS default period of 5 for daily price charts
anti_patterns.why: TA-Lib's BBANDS default period is 5 (very short); standard Bollinger Band practice uses 20 periods — always override the default

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use ATR stops without scaling to recent volatility regime
anti_patterns.why: A fixed ATR multiple (e.g., 2×) that worked in low-vol periods may be too tight in high-vol regimes; use NATR for regime-normalized sizing

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use CORREL as a static portfolio weight
anti_patterns.why: CORREL is a rolling statistic; correlations break down in crisis regimes — the lookback period must match the intended hedging horizon

anti_patterns.rule: Do not interpret BBANDS squeeze as a direction signal
anti_patterns.why: Bollinger squeeze only signals that a move is imminent, not its direction; combine with RSI or MACD for directional confirmation

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use NATR when Close prices include zero or null values
anti_patterns.why: Division by close price; zero close produces undefined output — filter or interpolate before computing

extensions.atr_smoothing_note: Wilder smoothing alpha = 1/n is less responsive than EMA alpha = 2/(n+1); ATR period 14 with Wilder ≈ EMA period 27
